steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the two sentences
steps.content:
The first sentence gives one reason Hilo soil is a fitting state soil: it is found in abundance across Hawaii. The blank begins a second sentence that adds another reason: it plays an important role in Hawaii's agriculture. Both sentences support the same conclusion, so the transition must signal addition, not contrast, alternatives, or time.
  • Choice A — Alternatively,: signals a different option or contrast — wrong, because the second sentence reinforces the first, not replaces it. ✗
  • Choice B — By contrast,: signals opposition — wrong, because the agricultural role of Hilo soil supports, not contradicts, its fitness as state soil. ✗
  • Choice C — At that time,: signals a time reference — wrong, because no specific time is being referenced in either sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — Additionally,: signals that a second supporting point is being added — correct, because the agricultural importance is a second reason layered onto the first. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the additive transition fits the passage structure
steps.content: “Additionally” introduces the second reason (agricultural importance) to support the claim that Hilo soil is a fitting state soil, exactly paralleling how the first reason (abundance) was introduced. That is the correct answer.
